ARTICLE_TITLE: Son Heung-min Leads Tottenham to Historic Champions League Semifinal Victory
ARTICLE_BODY: Tottenham Hotspur captain Son Heung-min delivered a man of the match performance as Spurs defeated Bayern Munich 3-1 in the Champions League semifinal second leg at Wembley Stadium. The South Korean forward scored twice and provided one assist, helping Tottenham overcome a 2-0 first-leg deficit to reach their first Champions League final since 2019. Son's spectacular 25-yard strike in the 73rd minute sealed the comeback victory that sent 70,000 fans into delirium. The 34-year-old striker has now scored 8 goals in this Champions League campaign, making him the tournament's joint top scorer. Tottenham manager Ange Postecoglou praised Son's leadership and determination throughout the knockout stages. The victory sets up a dream final against Manchester City at the Allianz Arena in Munich on May 31st, giving Tottenham their best chance to claim their first European trophy in decades.

A3: The Champions League final will take place on May 31st at the Allianz Arena in Munich.
